status: complete public-safe P3/P4 blast-radius map; no BEA launch or runtime proof
purpose: Turn the current beyond-two-player guard into an actionable map of the original-binary systems that must be proven or changed before any active P3/P4 gameplay attempt.

sourceStaticAnchors.capacityDeclaration: references/Onslaught/game.h:22 defines MAX_PLAYERS 4 and game.h:306-308 stores mPlayer/mController/mCurrentCamera arrays at MAX_PLAYERS capacity
sourceStaticAnchors.currentPlayerCountGate: references/Onslaught/game.cpp:705-708 sets mPlayers to 2 when WORLD.IsMultiplayer() is true, otherwise 1
sourceStaticAnchors.controllerAssignmentTrap: references/Onslaught/game.cpp:723-728 assigns player 0 from FRONTEND and then comments 'I don't support more than 2 players.' with ASSERT(i == 1)
sourceStaticAnchors.engineViewpointCeiling: references/Onslaught/engine.h:16 defines VIEWPOINTS 2; engine.h:70,93-94 stores mCamera/mViewport/mPlayer by VIEWPOINTS
sourceStaticAnchors.latentQuadSplitBranch: references/Onslaught/game.cpp:1793-1816 contains a 3/4-player quad-split branch with SetViewpoint(2/3), but this is not proof that retail runtime reaches it safely
sourceStaticAnchors.reconnectTwoSlotShape: references/Onslaught/game.cpp:1827-1828 renders RECONNECT_INTERFACE[0] and [1] only; game.cpp:1886-1892 gates unpause on players/controllers 0 and 1
sourceStaticAnchors.livesRespawnTwoSlotShape: references/Onslaught/game.h:300 stores mPlayer1Lives,mPlayer2Lives; game.cpp:3106-3162 and 3227-3234 switch on player 0/1 lives/respawn only
sourceStaticAnchors.frontendControllerShape: references/Onslaught/FrontEnd.cpp:444-448 returns player0 controller port; game.cpp:728 uses FEPMultiplayerStart.GetPlayer1ControllerPort for the second player

blastRadiusRows.finding: The source has four-slot game-side arrays for players, controllers, and current cameras.
blastRadiusRows.risk: Capacity is not activation. This cannot raise the proven original-binary runtime player count above two.
blastRadiusRows.beforeRuntimeAttempt: Verify retail layouts and all users of these arrays for P3/P4-safe indexing.

blastRadiusRows.finding: Current source setup derives multiplayer from WORLD/level state and assigns mPlayers=2, not 3 or 4.
blastRadiusRows.risk: A naive patch that only forces mPlayers above two would enter paths not proven safe in the retail binary.
blastRadiusRows.beforeRuntimeAttempt: Find or create a retail-safe player-count authority path and prove all downstream allocations/routes.

blastRadiusRows.finding: The setup path has explicit player-0/player-1 controller selection and an assert/comment against more than two players.
blastRadiusRows.risk: P3/P4 input routing cannot be inferred from session metadata or MAX_PLAYERS.
blastRadiusRows.beforeRuntimeAttempt: Map retail controller construction, frontend selection, options binding, and CPlayer input dispatch for P3/P4.

blastRadiusRows.finding: Game render code contains latent SetViewpoint(2/3) calls, but CEngine source storage is declared with VIEWPOINTS=2.
blastRadiusRows.risk: Quad-split source code alone may overrun or alias retail engine viewpoint storage if activated blindly.
blastRadiusRows.beforeRuntimeAttempt: Prove retail CEngine storage/callsites can hold more than two viewpoints or design a non-splitscreen P3/P4 representation.

blastRadiusRows.finding: Reconnect handling is visibly shaped around interface slots 0 and 1.
blastRadiusRows.risk: P3/P4 controller loss/reconnect behavior would be undefined without additional mapping or replacement UX.
blastRadiusRows.beforeRuntimeAttempt: Define and prove P3/P4 disconnect policy, including whether extra slots are gameplay, spectator, or remote-only.

blastRadiusRows.finding: Lives and respawn/result logic are P1/P2-shaped in source.
blastRadiusRows.risk: Co-op/versus semantics cannot be scaled by transport alone; P3/P4 deaths and win/loss rules need proof or redesign.
blastRadiusRows.beforeRuntimeAttempt: Map retail death/respawn/win paths and decide whether P3/P4 are active players, spectators, or external clients.

blastRadiusRows.finding: Per-frame controller flush loops scale with mPlayers.
blastRadiusRows.risk: This is an enabler only after player count, controller allocation, and engine storage are proven safe.
blastRadiusRows.beforeRuntimeAttempt: Use exact-PID CDB to prove P3/P4 CController and CPlayer receive paths before any gameplay claim.

blastRadiusRows.area: Gameplay systems that branch on multiplayer
blastRadiusRows.finding: Several gameplay/render/camera paths alter behavior for multiplayer generally, not for an arbitrary player count.
blastRadiusRows.risk: P3/P4 may inherit two-player assumptions in camera, render LOD, target/audio/HUD behavior, and collision/display tuning.
blastRadiusRows.beforeRuntimeAttempt: Audit retail multiplayer branches for exact dependence on mPlayers, viewpoint index, player pointer, and controller slot.

hardBlockersBeforeP3P4Runtime: Retail CEngine viewpoint storage is not proven above two viewpoints.
Current player-count setup does not produce mPlayers=3 or mPlayers=4.
Controller assignment has explicit P1/P2 logic and an assert/comment against more than two players.
Reconnect UI and controller-disconnect policy are P1/P2-shaped.
Lives, respawn, and result flows are P1/P2-shaped.
P3/P4 CPlayer__ReceiveButtonActionState rows have not been observed in copied retail runtime.
No visible or state proof exists for more than two active original-binary players.
No co-op/versus/team/spectator runtime mode semantics are proven for more than two active original-binary slots.
runtimeAttemptPrerequisites: fresh retail Ghidra/decompile/xref evidence for mPlayers assignment and all direct users before any patch proposal
retail layout proof for CGame player/controller/camera arrays and CEngine viewpoint storage
exact-PID CDB proof of P3/P4 controller allocation and routing, if attempting runtime activation
exact-PID CDB proof of P3/P4 CPlayer__ReceiveButtonActionState rows
P1/P2 regression proof after any P3/P4-oriented change
visual or state proof for more than two active viewpoints or a documented non-splitscreen representation
death/respawn/result policy proof for the intended mode family
public-safe docs/checkers that keep same-host, second-host, matchmaking, and native-netcode claims separate
requiredNewProofClassForP3P4: fresh retail static map for mPlayers, player/controller/camera arrays, CEngine viewpoint storage, HUD/layout, frontend selection, result/death/respawn flows, and render loops
retail Ghidra/decompile/xref evidence that any patched or rebuilt original-binary profile can safely allocate and route more than two active players
copied-runtime exact-PID CDB evidence for P3/P4 controller dispatch and CPlayer__ReceiveButtonActionState rows
copied-runtime visual or state evidence for more than two active viewpoints or an intentional non-splitscreen P3/P4 representation
explicit regression that P1/P2 behavior remains intact
updated mode proof for co-op, free-for-all, team-versus, or spectator/admin semantics before any mode claim
nextProofLadder: same-host session-control proof for join-ticket lifetime/replay/reconnect/spectator-admin metadata
real second-host private command-source proof when a separate physical/private host is available
retail Ghidra P3/P4 blast-radius deep dive for CEngine/CGame/controller/result paths before any runtime activation
only after those gates, copied-runtime P3/P4 allocation/input observation in an app-owned safe copy
